address: Sukhaphiban Road, Wat Mai, Mueang Chanthaburi, Chanthaburi 22000
best_time_to_visit: Late afternoon (15:00-17:00) when the light is soft, temperatures drop, and the pastel shophouses photograph beautifully

description.en:
## Overview

The Chanthaboon Waterfront Community is a beautifully preserved riverside neighborhood of pastel-colored shophouses stretching along the Chanthaburi River. This walkable heritage quarter — officially promoted as an art and cultural conservation area — offers a rare glimpse into the multicultural history of eastern Thailand, where Thai, Vietnamese, and Chinese communities have coexisted for over a century. It feels worlds apart from Bangkok's chaos.

## History

The waterfront community dates back over 300 years, originally settled by Vietnamese Catholics fleeing religious persecution and Chinese merchants drawn by the gem and fruit trades. During the French occupation (1893-1905), colonial-era architectural details were layered onto the existing shophouses — shuttered windows, wrought-iron balconies, and faded painted signs in Thai, Vietnamese, and French. The community underwent careful restoration in the 2010s, converting derelict buildings into cafes, galleries, and heritage museums while preserving the original facades.

## What to See

The main walking route takes about 20 minutes end to end. Key stops include Baan Luang Rajamaitri Historic Inn — a 150-year-old wooden house now serving as a small museum with artifacts from Chanthaburi's trading past. The Chanthaburi River Community Cultural Center (free, open 10:30-16:00) provides deeper context on the area's history. Independent cafes in converted shophouses serve excellent local coffee and desserts. At the end of the lane, a small bridge leads directly to the Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception. Look for the Rocket Ice Cream shop, which uses the province's original ice cream-making machine.

## Practical Info

The waterfront is free to explore and accessible at any time, though shops and cafes generally open between 10:00 and 18:00. The area is compact and best explored on foot — wear comfortable shoes for the narrow lanes. It is a 13-minute walk (1.1 km) from the Chanthaburi Bus Station. Try the local specialty kuay teow tom yam tha le (spicy and sour seafood noodles) at one of the small restaurants near House No.69.
description.nl: Een prachtig bewaard gebleven riviergemeenschap van pastelkleurige winkelpanden met Frans-Vietnamese architectonische details — ramen met luiken, balkons met frangipani en vervaagde geschilderde tekens in het Thais en Vietnamees. De smalle steegjes herbergen onafhankelijke cafes, kleine kunstgalerieën en oude handelshuizen die het verhaal vertellen van het multiculturele erfgoed van de stad. Het beste te voet te verkennen in het late middaglicht.

tips: Walk the full 20-minute route and cross the footbridge at the end to reach the Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception
Visit the free Chanthaburi River Community Cultural Center (open 10:30-16:00) for context on the area's multicultural history
Try kuay teow tom yam tha le (spicy seafood noodles) — the local signature dish — at a shophouse restaurant near House No.69
